---
title: Young firms lead job creation across the US
description: Grouped bar chart showing job creation by establishment age for rural
  and nonrural areas.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-job-creation-grouped-bar/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Entrepreneurship
  - Labor Market
tags:
  - BDS
  - Job Creation
  - Firm Age
  - Entrepreneurship
  - Rural vs. Nonrural
  - Bar Chart
image: job_creation_grouped_bar.svg
chart:
  title: Young firms lead job creation across the US
  chartType: bar
  interactive: false
  dataSource: U.S. Census Bureau, Business Dynamics Statistics
  sourceUrl: https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/bds.html
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - census-bds
usesPackages:
  - cori-data-bds
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This grouped bar chart breaks down job creation by establishment age, showing that young firms are disproportionately responsible for net job creation.](./job_creation_grouped_bar.svg)

## Overview

This grouped bar chart breaks down job creation by establishment age, showing that young firms are disproportionately responsible for net job creation.

## Key Findings

- Youngest establishments (0-5 years) create the most jobs per establishment
- Job creation rates decline with firm age
- The pattern is consistent across rural and nonrural areas

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/job_creation_grouped_bar.R` in the producing project.
